entry.title: iniBuilds Tristar Airliner to Get Update “by Next Week”
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently submitted a new version of their Tristar Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024 to the Marketplace, bringing a further round of fixes and improvements across core systems, set to become available next week. Version 1.0.2 focuses on resolving key areas such as INS behavior, autopilot and flight director logic, ILS functionality, and various cockpit interactions, as well as general stability and usability enhancements. The complete changelog follows below: Systems ‍ FIXED Fuel used reset function is INOP FIXED RMI incorrectly points to ILS stations FIXED ALT ARM sometimes fails to engage FIXED PMS exceeds VMO in POD version FIXED AP overshoots altitude in TM/VNAV mode ‍ Art ‍ ADDED Engine smoke FX during startup ADDED Cockpit registration placard assets FIXED High VRAM usage on 8 GB GPUs ‍ PFB ‍ ADDED OFP re-download functionality ADDED keyboard input lock option ADDED 7-sec timeout to PFB text field entry focus ‍ Sounds ‍ FIXED Audio loss when switching camera views FIXED Missing cabin altitude warning horn FIXED Missing fire warning test aural alerts ‍ The add-on is available in the MSFS Marketplace for $49.99 If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Deltawing Simulations Teases Upcoming ATR-72 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Deltawing Simulations has recently teased their progress on their ATR-72 rendition for X-Plane 12, showcasing the modeling work around the flight deck and providing a brief update on the overall progress. They claim most of the essential systems are now complete, and they have recently shifted their main focus to visual aspects, such as displays and how they work, as well as cockpit buttons and warning indicators. They plan to release the add-on sometime in 2026, but the exact date and pricing have yet to be revealed. External previews will be shown in later development updates. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Mystery Developer Rhodiumcode Reveals Surprise A350 Project for MSFS
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Practically out of nowhere, Rhodiumcode, a new studio from the public perspective, has today announced that it is developing an Airbus A350 for Microsoft Flight Simulator, with a first cinematic trailer set to premiere on May 3 at 0500Z. The announcement first came in a Reddit post, where the team introduced themselves as “a small studio of developers and artists” working on their first aircraft for Microsoft Flight Simulator. According to Rhodiumcode, the project has been in development for the past three years, obviously entirely behind the scenes. This surprise announcement is, perhaps, similar to what we saw with Vector’s 787 reveal just a few months ago. In their first message, Rhodiumcode says it has been building “their own vision” of the A350 from the ground up, and confirmed that the first proper look at the aircraft will come in the form of a trailer premiering on Sunday, May 3. They followed up with a more detailed Discord announcement from Michael, identified as president and lead developer of Rhodiumcode. He again describes the company as a small game studio composed of enthusiasts from diverse backgrounds. Michael says he has been flying in simulators since the FS2004 era and that the May 3 trailer will follow the aircraft on an early-morning departure from Taipei. He made it clear that Rhodiumcode is still early in its journey, that there is “a long road ahead”, and that the studio is not yet making promises about features or timelines. ‍ They do not add much more, saying only that they want to deliver a “sophisticated and immersive” A350 experience. At the time of writing, we do not know much more about Rhodiumcode beyond what the studio has now shared. Their website describes them as a small California-based game studio, and their YouTube channel dates back to 2022. Rhodiumcode also opened a help-wanted channel on Discord, inviting both type-rated pilots and simmers to get involved in the project. Join their discord here. We will keep you updated on future developments. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Grinelli Designs Shares Further Previews of F-100D Super Sabre for DCS
entry.summary: Sim: DCS. Grinelli Designs have recently released a development update for their upcoming F-100D Super Sabre module for DCS, which focused on the art, flight model and sound development. The North American F-100D Super Sabre was one of the pioneers of American military aviation, as it was the first jet fighter in the USAF that was capable of achieving supersonic speeds in level flight. Developed from the F-86 Sabre, it entered service in 1954 and was most famous for its use during the Vietnam War. According to the developers, the art development is almost finished, as they have used photogrammetry scans of an actual F-100D during the process of 3D modelling. They have also confirmed that the module will feature various historically accurate liveries of both USAF training squadrons and the squadrons that flew during the Vietnam War. Moving on to the flight model development, Grinelli Designs is working in collaboration with Eagle Dynamics to fine-tune the flight model, as the F-100D was known for its stunning high-speed performance, but also for its behaviour in low-speed conditions. The audio development is also nearing completion, with the developing team using sound recordings from a real F-100. Eagle Dynamics has also confirmed that the module is currently undergoing internal testing. ‍ Currently, there is no information about the release date or the pricing, but as always, Threshold will keep you updated on any future updates! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Flight Panels Updates PMDG 737 Stream Deck Profile
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Flight Panels has announced a new version of its PMDG 737 Stream Deck profile, rebuilding the product ahead of the continued PMDG 737 rollout for MSFS 2024. Originally released in 2023, the profile has now grown from 312 controls across 12 pages to 841 controls across 33 pages. According to Flight Panels, this version covers the full cockpit to a greater extent than the old profile. Flight Panels says the new Generation 2 version now includes cockpit and cabin feature controls, a full MCDU with keyboard and numpad, an audio control panel, and much broader system access across the aircraft. The profile supports both 15-key and 32-key layouts, making it compatible with the Stream Deck XL, MK.2, Stream Deck Mobile, and Corsair Xeneon Edge. Separate autopilot profiles are also available for the Stream Deck +, which can be accessed via its buttons, touchscreen, and rotary dials. The product works with both MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024, and the Generation 2 update is free for existing customers. New customers can pick it up from the Flight Panels store for approximately $34 USD. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FSDreamTeam Updates GSX with Towing Capability
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. FSDreamTeam has released GSX update 3.9.1, adding towing support. GSX can now move aircraft with both push-in and gate-to-gate towing, allowing them to be repositioned between gates and towed from the taxiway to a stand. When taxiing in and selecting a parking position, GSX can now send a towbarless tug out to meet the aircraft and tow it from the taxiway to the gate, using an automatically selected route along the airport’s taxiway layout. If you are already parked, GSX can now push the aircraft out using the existing pushback route, then tow it across to another gate, with the tug again choosing the best pushback direction for the destination. In terms of fixes, most menus now include a proper back button, pushback speed is now adjustable via a slider in the settings, and seated passenger support for the PMDG 737-700 in MSFS 2024 has been added. Version 3.9.1 – April 11th, 2026 ‍ Full changelog You can buy a copy of GSX Pro here. Version 3.9.1 can be installed via the FSDT Installer. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Heatblur Simulations Teases F-4E Phantom II for Microsoft Flight Simulator
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Heatblur Simulations has recently given an update on its McDonnell Douglas F-4 Phantom II project for Microsoft Flight Simulator, which is now said to be entering final testing. As pre-release development wraps up, Heatblur says it is putting the final finishing touches in place before starting the release process, which is expected to be completed by April. This work includes work on various input systems, EFB pages, Navigraph integration and more. The F-4E Phantom project is based on Heatblur’s Anvil component-based simulation framework, aiming to recreate the aircraft in authentic, high-detail form. The F4 will include a fully custom external flight model that represents the real aircraft's flight envelope and behaviours in detail, ensuring the in-sim recreation behaves as closely as possible to the real-life counterpart. They say they are working on a release trailer, and it does look like the F-4E Phantom II for MSFS is close to release. We will keep you updated on future developments. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Nimbus Simulation Studios Further Teases 707 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Nimbus Simulation Studios has once again come out on Facebook to share some more images of their upcoming 707 project for X-Plane 12. The previews showcase the aircraft on the ground and in the air around Albuquerque. We still do not know much else about the project, but the team says that they are about 95% done with the external model, texturing and animations. They also added that another preview focusing on the cockpit will be shared sometime next week (13APR-19APR). We will keep an eye on progress! ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently updated their rendition of the TriStar Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing a further round of fixes and improvements across core systems. Version 1.0.2 focuses on resolving key areas, including INS behavior, autopilot and flight director logic, ILS functionality, and various cockpit interactions, as well as general stability and usability enhancements. The complete changelog follows below: ‍ V1.0.2SYSTEMS ⚙️ ART 🎨 PFB📜 SOUNDS🔊 The add-on is available in the Marketplace for $49.99 If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: SoFly Updates Cardiff Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. SoFly has recently issued an update to their rendition of Cardiff Airport (EGFF) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, adding animated 3D people, performance enhancements, fixes to photogrammetry issues, and more. ​ Update v1.2.0 brings a major revamp of their scenery product, with improved performance across the board, a smaller file size, mitigation of all photogrammetry issues, removal of ground bumps/lumps, animated 3D passengers, and a custom GSX profile. The complete changelog follows below: It’s available on Contrail for roughly $12.63 (30% off), requiring at least 2.92 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: MK Studios Updates Porto Airport V2 for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MK Studios has recently issued an update to their newly released Porto Airport (LPPR) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, bringing AFCAD fixes, minor tweaks, and more. Update v2.0.1 - the first so far since its release -brings AFCAD fixes, fixes certain missing procedures, adds the ground frequency, fixes the departures sign, adds custom buildings near runway 17, and adds lights to the train station. ​The scenery is available on Contrail for roughly $20.44, and V1 owners are entitled to a 20% discount coupon. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Updates Düsseldorf Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has recently issued an update to their rendition of Düsseldorf Airport (EDDL) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, adding new buildings, AFCAD adjustments, terrain fixes, and more. Update v1.0.3 adds the new fire station, new solar farms to the north of the runway, reworks ground markings to match the current layout, adjusts AFCAD parkings and taxiway paths, changes some service vehicle textures, changes taxisigns to the current layout, fixes a terrain glitch on the southeast apron, and tweaks the ground orthoimagery. It’s available on Aerosoft’s shop for roughly $15.99 (20% off), requiring at least 4.74 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: LAND3 Simulations Updates vRAAS for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. LAND3 Simulations has recently issued a major update to their vRAAS add-on for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, adding a realistic voice pack, ROW/ROPS (Airbus) & RSAT (Boeing), multiple fixes, and more. Update v1.3.0 brings a new realistic voice pack, ROW/ROPS (Airbus) and RSAT (Boeing), “Autobrake OFF” callout on Airbus, adds a debug page, improves the timing for distance remaining callouts and altimeter setting tolerances, disables the 1500m and 1200m callouts by default, fixes the auto-start not working for some users, and mitigates the audio device error. It’s available on Contrail for roughly $16.83, requiring at least 338 MB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FlightFactor's 777-300ER Expansion to Release on Thursday
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. FlightFactor has recently defined a release date for their long-awaited 777-300ER expansion for X-Plane 12, set to land on Thursday (16/04) for $89. The longest member of the 777 family adds to their pre-existing 777v2 lineup, bringing more range, capability, and route variety, costing only $30 to 777V2 owners. It features the GE90-115BL engines as standard, and owners of the engine expansion upgrade will also get access to the GE90-115B. ​ Selected streamers will receive an early access key to showcase the product within the next few days. ​If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: FlightFactor Updates 777v2 for X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. FlightFactor has recently issued an update to their 777v2 for X-Plane 12, bringing a variety of new features and fixes in preparation for the 777-300ER release on Thursday. ​ Update 2.5.3 adds the option to disable the analog clock, as per newer MSNs, adds the modern CDU panel and modern compass options, adds a music option, improves CPDLC/ACARS stability and operations, improves support for multi-monitor configurations, and several other fixes. The complete changelog follows below: - added no analog clock option - added modern cdu panel option - added modern compass option - added music option - improved CPDLC/ACARS stability/operations - improved multi-monitor configurations support - fine tuned backlights/instruments/displays brt things according to the references from real plane - fixed cases where closed OS popup displays appear empty after next plane loading - fixed ACARS systems not working after first successful request on macOS/Linux - fixed alternate switching on of C1/C2 demand pumps during movement Flaps - fixed issue in OPT when hg and hPa determined incorrectly - fixed WX RDR panel tooltips text - fixed various 2D window popups (related to procedures, crew operations and tooltips) having wrong position in specific multi-monitor configurations - fixed Available and Operational numbers swapped on EFB MEL page - fixed possible fmc soft crash when the vnav cdu crz page is active and spd limit mode is active - fixed fpa is displayed on the ground on des vnav page - fixed can't access cockpit via gate - updated the remote CDU, a new generation added ‍ The add-on is available on the X-Plane.Org Store for roughly $89.00, requiring at least 7 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Official Trailer for the FlightFactor 777-300ER Now Out
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Ahead of the anticipated product launch tomorrow, Apr. 16, FlightFactor has now released the official trailer movie for their Boeing 777-300ER, coming to X-Plane 12. Uploaded via the X-Plane.org Store YouTube Channel, and produced by Fly777X Aviation, we see what is likely very close to a finished release candidate flying on the big screen. The 777-300ER is part of FlightFactor’s 777V2 series for X-Plane 12, aiming to rebuild their original 777 line-up from scratch, in very high detail. The release cycle began with the 777-200ER last year, followed by the 777F, with the long-awaited 300ER now imminent as well. It is scheduled to release tomorrow, Thursday Apr. 16, although FlightFactor has not yet confirmed a specific release time. For a closer look at the aircraft, you can take a look at one of the numerous pre-release streams broadcast over the last few days by several content creators. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!
